text: Welcome to Fragmented, a software developer podcast where we talk about building good software and becoming better developers.

text: Hey everybody, I wanted to take a quick minute before we get going to let you know about the new YouTube channel that I recently started.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: On this channel, I teach software developers and mobile developers and specifically, how to become independent consultants.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: If you've ever wanted to become an independent consultant but weren't sure how, then this is the channel for you.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: I'll talk about the billing rates that you need to set, how to find clients, how to find an evergreen source of clients,

text: how to stay on new technology trends, how to promote yourself, how to market yourself, how to get insurance, you name it.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: If it's around working for yourself and independent consulting and software, I'll be talking about it.

text: Now, when you're at a standing desk, what you're going to find is if you haven't done this, you're going to find you get tired because you're burning calories, you're standing up.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So there's a couple of ways to combat this to hurt.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: You know, sometimes your back will hurt.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Sometimes your legs will hurt.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So number one, wear shoes.

text: Sounds, you know, most people think, well, I'm at home, why should I wear shoes?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: But if you're at home, wear shoes.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: They're padded and you'll actually feel a lot better when you wear your shoes at a standing desk.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Furthermore, I recommend that you buy an anti-fatigue mat.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Now you can buy one from Amazon.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: There's a ton of options out there.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: When I first did my first standing desk close to 10 years ago, there was not really many options out there.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Nowadays, there's all kinds of options on Amazon.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: You can find one that you like.

text: Personally, I use the Ergo Driven Topo Mat and it has a bunch of ridges and bumps on it.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And then what you can do is I might be standing there with my shoes on for a while.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: But then I can put my heel on it or my toes on a certain ridge, stretch out my calf, stretch out different parts of my leg.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: I can even actually take my shoes off and put the middle of my foot onto like a kind of a point, a soft point that they have and stretch out various parts of my foot while I'm standing there.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So these anti-fatigue mats will help you last a lot longer during the day.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Now, how long should you be standing?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: I recommend that you stand no more than 70% of the day.

text: Some people prefer to stand all day.

text: There's some science out there that says you shouldn't stand all day.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: I don't know.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: For me, I try to stand anywhere between 50 to 70% of the day.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: 70% is my goal.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So definitely get an anti-fatigue mat that you can stand on when you're standing.

text: Helps a lot.

text: Makes you feel a lot better.

text: Now, also, when you're standing, I recommend that you, if you have like a footstool or something that's a footstool height, put it next to your desk and then put one foot on that when you're standing.
